TEXT.citation: Wikipedia contributors. (2026). Sakizaya Wikipedia. Wikimedia Foundation. Retrieved 2026-06-09, from https://szy.wikipedia.org/
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @misc{Wiki_szy, title = {Sakizaya Wikipedia}, author = {{Wikipedia contributors}}, year = {2026}, publisher = {Wikimedia Foundation}, url = {https://szy.wikipedia.org/}, urldate = {2026-06-09} }

S.FORM: == Ali Bongo Ondimba == u ngangan niza u ngangan nu Hulam: ťėŅťáĆ¬∑ťā¶śąą¬∑ÁŅĀšłĀŚ∑ī u ngangan nu Lipun: „āĘ„É™„Éľ„ÉĽ„Éú„É≥„āī„ÉĽ„ā™„É≥„Éá„ā£„É≥„Éź u ngangan nu Amilika: Ali Bongo Ondimba u siwkay niza tatama, 2 a bulad 9 a demiad, Ali Bongo Ondimba, u misingkiway. 1959 a mihcan nalecuhan u tabakiay a tapang nu kanatal (congtung) nu Gabon ayza sa ci Ali Bongo Ondimba, micakat a demiad sa i 2009 a mihca 10 bulad 16 demiad. napahezek ciniza i Pali sakacacay tabakiay a cacudadan. cyama niza u Cungtung tu. 1989-1991 mamikuwan ciniza ku palcabayay a Pu Pucang. 1999-2009 mamikuwan ku palangatay a Pu Pucang. kakuniza u nasulitan nu Hulam atu Amilika Ali Bongo Ondimba is a Gabonese politician who has been President of Gabon since October 2009. Bongo is the son of Omar Bongo, who was President of Gabon from 1967 until his death in 2009. During his father's presidency, he was Minister of Foreign Affairs from 1989 to 1991 and represented Bongoville as a Deputy in the National Assembly from 1991 to 1999; subsequently he was Minister of Defense from 1999 to 2009. Following his father's death after 41 years in power, he was first elected in the August 2009 presidential election. He was re-elected in August 2016, in elections marred by numerous irregularities, arrests, human rights violations and post-election violence. Bongo is also President of the PDG.
S.PHON: == ar~…ĺi boŇčo ondimba == u ŇčaŇčan niza u ŇčaŇčan nu ńßur~…ĺam: ********* u ŇčaŇčan nu r~…ĺipun: ************** u ŇčaŇčan nu amir~…ĺika: ar~…ĺi boŇčo ondimba u siwkaj niza tatama, * a bur~…ĺad * a d…ômiad, ar~…ĺi boŇčo ondimba, u misiŇčkiwaj. **** a mińß ¶an nar~…ĺ…ô ¶uńßan u tabakiaj a tapaŇč nu kanatar~…ĺ ( ¶oŇčtuŇč) nu gabon ajza sa ¶i ar~…ĺi boŇčo ondimba, mi ¶akat a d…ômiad sa i **** a mińß ¶a ** bur~…ĺad ** d…ômiad. napańß…ôz…ôk ¶iniza i par~…ĺi saka ¶a ¶aj tabakiaj a ¶a ¶udadan. ¶jama niza u ¶uŇčtuŇč tu. ****-**** mamikuwan ¶iniza ku par~…ĺ ¶abajaj a pu pu ¶aŇč. ****-**** mamikuwan ku par~…ĺaŇčataj a pu pu ¶aŇč. kakuniza u nasur~…ĺitan nu ńßur~…ĺam atu amir~…ĺika ar~…ĺi boŇčo ondimba is a gabon…ôs…ô por~…ĺiti ¶ian wńßo ńßas b…ô…ôn pr…ôsid…ônt of gabon sin ¶…ô o ¶tob…ôr ****. boŇčo is tńß…ô son of omar boŇčo, wńßo was pr…ôsid…ônt of gabon from **** untir~…ĺ ńßis d…ôatńß in ****. duriŇč ńßis fatńß…ôr Ē~ °s pr…ôsid…ôn ¶j, ńß…ô was minist…ôr of for…ôign affairs from **** to **** and r…ôpr…ôs…ônt…ôd boŇčo*ir~…ĺr~…ĺ…ô as a d…ôputj in tńß…ô nationar~…ĺ ass…ômbr~…ĺj from **** to ****; subs…ô*u…ôntr~…ĺj ńß…ô was minist…ôr of d…ôf…ôns…ô from **** to ****. for~…ĺr~…ĺowiŇč ńßis fatńß…ôr Ē~ °s d…ôatńß aft…ôr ** j…ôars in pow…ôr, ńß…ô was first …ôr~…ĺ…ô ¶t…ôd in tńß…ô august **** pr…ôsid…ôntiar~…ĺ …ôr~…ĺ…ô ¶tion. ńß…ô was r…ô-…ôr~…ĺ…ô ¶t…ôd in august ****, in …ôr~…ĺ…ô ¶tions marr…ôd bj num…ôrous irr…ôgur~…ĺariti…ôs, arr…ôsts, ńßuman rigńßts *ior~…ĺations and post-…ôr~…ĺ…ô ¶tion *ior~…ĺ…ôn ¶…ô. boŇčo is ar~…ĺso pr…ôsid…ônt of tńß…ô pdg.
S.FORM: == Ali Bongo Ondimba == u ngangan niza u ngangan nu Hulam: ťėŅťáĆ¬∑ťā¶śąą¬∑ÁŅĀšłĀŚ∑ī u ngangan nu Lipun: „āĘ„É™„Éľ„ÉĽ„Éú„É≥„āī„ÉĽ„ā™„É≥„Éá„ā£„É≥„Éź u ngangan nu Amilika: Ali Bongo Ondimba u siwkay niza tatama, 2 a bulad 9 a demiad, Ali Bongo Ondimba, u misingkiway. 1959 a mihcan nalecuhan u tabakiay a tapang nu kanatal (congtung) nu Gabon ayza sa ci Ali Bongo Ondimba, micakat a demiad sa i 2009 a mihca 10 bulad 16 demiad. napahezek ciniza i Pali sakacacay tabakiay a cacudadan. cyama niza u Cungtung tu. 1989-1991 mamikuwan ciniza ku palcabayay a Pu Pucang. 1999-2009 mamikuwan ku palangatay a Pu Pucang. kakuniza u nasulitan nu Hulam atu Amilika Ali Bongo Ondimba is a Gabonese politician who has been President of Gabon since October 2009. Bongo is the son of Omar Bongo, who was President of Gabon from 1967 until his death in 2009. During his father's presidency, he was Minister of Foreign Affairs from 1989 to 1991 and represented Bongoville as a Deputy in the National Assembly from 1991 to 1999; subsequently he was Minister of Defense from 1999 to 2009. Following his father's death after 41 years in power, he was first elected in the August 2009 presidential election. He was re-elected in August 2016, in elections marred by numerous irregularities, arrests, human rights violations and post-election violence. Bongo is also President of the PDG.
